[Cover 1]

Name:
Eternal Atake 
[Cover 2]
Notes: Second cover shown for the 2020 Eternal Atake and would be referred to as "Album cover 2". A Poll was ran the same day it was revealed, and ended up making two polls, winning both at 62% of each polls votes. The one revealed has slight differences from the current one, most noticeably being less shine on Planet Uzi.

Name:
Eternal Atake
[Cover 3]
Notes: Third and final cover revealed for the 2020 Eternal Atake and would be referred to as "Album cover 3". A poll was ran the same day it was revealed, and ended up making two polls, getting 2nd place in both with 32% of the votes in the first poll, then 38% of the votes in the second. This cover would later be revised without any signs.

Name:
Eternal Atake
[Cover 3, No Signs]

Name: That Way
Notes: Cover for the song That Way, which later sparked a legal feud between Uzi's team and the artist who created the inspiration for this cover. The original artist didn't like the way their art had been used, and according to Artxstic, he hadn't known who the art belonged to, due to the reference image being low quality to the point where he couldn't see any watermarks. Uzi would end up sorting it out with the original artist, so the cover could stay up.

Name: Barter 16
Notes: The cover revealed for what would've been Uzi's 4th studio album, "Barter 16". Was first teased on Uzi's IG on Young Thug's birthday, captioned "Happy birthday @thuggerthugger1 Barter 16 🅱️ soon 🩸 Love You 💚" . The cover is an exact reference to Young Thug's 2015 mixtape Barter 6, hence the name for Uzi's being Barter 16

Name:
Barter 16
[Farris' Version]
Notes: Alternative cover done by Farris, which is still currently up for sale on his inprnt store titled "b16 throwaway". Unknown if it was ever gonna be used as a cover for the album during it's creation, but it's safe to say it was likely gonna be used as an alternative.
